h2h_summary: Korea Republic and Czechia have never officially met before, making their 2026 World Cup Group A clash a historic first encounter between the two nations.
team_style_home: Korea Republic play a high-intensity pressing 4-2-3-1 system under Hong Myung-bo, emphasising rapid vertical transitions, attacking width through the full-backs, and unleashing pace through Son Heung-min and the wide attackers on the counter.
team_style_away: Czechia operate a pragmatic 3-4-2-1 under veteran coach Miroslav Koubek, prioritising defensive solidity, deep blocks out of possession, and lethal set-piece delivery — 11 of their 22 qualifying goals came from dead-ball situations.
wc_history_home: Korea Republic have appeared at 12 World Cups and famously reached the semi-finals as co-hosts in 2002, becoming the only Asian nation ever to reach the last four of the tournament.
wc_history_away: Czechia (as Czechoslovakia) reached the World Cup final twice — in 1934 and 1962 — and as an independent Czech Republic qualified for 2006 before returning in 2026 after a 20-year absence, qualifying via two dramatic penalty shootout victories in the playoffs.
group_context: Group A features co-host Mexico, South Korea, Czechia, and South Africa, with Mexico and South Korea considered the favourites to advance; Korea Republic vs Czechia is effectively an early battle for the second automatic qualification spot, making a positive opening result crucial for both sides.
strengths_home: World-class individual quality through Son Heung-min and a technically gifted attacking line
High-intensity pressing and fast vertical transitions disrupt organised defences
Unbeaten through AFC qualifying — tournament experience and winning mentality
strengths_away: Elite defensive organisation and resilience — proven ability to grind results under pressure
Devastating set-piece threat; scored 11 of 22 qualifying goals from dead balls
Strong club chemistry with 10 Slavia Prague players forming a cohesive defensive block
